name: GridPP Tier-1 (RAL)
oneLiner: The UK's Tier-1 LHC computing centre, offering secure large-scale CPU and storage capacity for mission workloads.
summary: GridPP is the UK's contribution to the Worldwide LHC Computing Grid and aggregates computing across national labs and universities. The Tier-1 centre at Rutherford Appleton Laboratory provides advanced processors, secure operations, extensive storage, and high-speed networking for particle physics data processing. For AI inference it is most relevant as a mission-grade platform for embarrassingly parallel workloads, data staging, and large-scale CPU processing rather than dense accelerator-based model serving.

keyFacts.hardware: Tier-1 CPU and storage infrastructure at RAL, plus a wider distributed GridPP network
keyFacts.performance: Thousands of CPU cores and petabytes of storage available for large parallel data workflows
keyFacts.energy: Operated as a mission-critical research service with secure data centre practices

access.route: Access is organised through GridPP and STFC; up to 10% of resources can be made available to new communities, including industry.
